title: Write initial boot prompt for execute_tactus (≤2k tokens)
description:
# Write initial boot prompt for execute_tactus

## What

The first complete draft of the tool-description / boot-prompt that every MCP client will receive for the new `execute_tactus` tool. Used by the spike to validate models can author Plexus-flavored Tactus Lua from this alone (plus on-demand docs).

## Hard constraint

≤2000 tokens, measured with the target frontier models' tokenizers. (5k is the production ceiling per Epic 8; 2k is the spike's tighter constraint to leave headroom.)

## Required content

- Tool name, one-paragraph description.
- The single most important rule: `local plexus = require('plexus')` then use `plexus.*`.
- Discovery: `plexus.docs.list()` and `plexus.docs.get(key)`.
- Cost / budget: `plexus.budget.remaining()`, `plexus.budget.with{}`.
- HITL: destructive ops auto-approve; `no_confirm = true` to bypass.
- Error semantics: `{ ok, value, error }` shape.
- 2–3 minimal canonical examples (read, predict, evaluation invocation).

## What it must NOT do

- Inline the API for every namespace. Use `plexus.docs.get` for that.
- Inline cookbooks for common workflows. Same.
- Be verbose. Aim for cognitive density.

## Deliverable

`spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/boot_prompt.md` with token counts measured and recorded.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Progress: drafted initial `execute_tactus` boot prompt at `spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/boot_prompt.md`.

Content covers:
- `local plexus = require("plexus")`
- `plexus.docs.{list,get}` and `plexus.api.list` discovery
- cost-discipline ladder
- `plexus.budget.remaining()`
- table-argument convention
- HITL-by-default for destructive operations
- structured error guidance
- examples for score discovery, single prediction, synchronous evaluation, and async evaluation handles

Token measurement:
- chars=3189
- words=408
- estimated_tokens_char4=797
- tiktoken_cl100k=772

This is comfortably below the spike limit of 2000 tokens and the production hard ceiling of 5000 tokens.

Task remains `in_progress` pending review/landing.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Added an enforceable boot-prompt token gate to the spike harness.

Changes:
- `harness.py` now defines `BOOT_PROMPT_TOKEN_LIMIT = 2000`.
- `--validate-tasks` validates `boot_prompt.md` exists and stays within the token cap.
- Validation output now includes `boot_prompt_tokens` and `boot_prompt_token_limit`.
- README smoke-test expectations now include the boot-prompt token check.

Verification:
```bash
python -m py_compile "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/harness.py"
python "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/harness.py" --validate-tasks
python "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/harness.py" --all --model stub-oracle --run-id ignored-bootprompt-verify
```

Result:
- Current boot prompt: 772 estimated tokens / 2000 limit.
- Stub oracle: 15/15 passed.
